---
title: "Immune priming effects of prior influenza vaccination on subsequent vaccine response"
format:
  poster-typst:
    size: "40x22.5"
    poster-authors: "Murphy John^, Ted Ross*, Andrea Sant†, Andreas Handel^"
    departments: "^Department of Epidemiology and Biostatistics, University of Georgia, Athens, GA, USA · *Cleveland Clinic Florida Research & Innovation Center, Port St. Lucie, FL, USA · †Department of Microbiology and Immunology, University of Rochester, Rochester, NY, USA"
    poster-banner: "figures/poster-banner.svg"
    header-text-color: "111111"
    show-footer: "false"
    footer-color: "BA0C2F"
    num-columns: 1
    body-font-size: 11
    title-row-size: 4.15
    title-font-size: 30
    authors-font-size: 15
    department-font-size: 9
    margin-top: 0.25
    margin-left: 0.55
    margin-right: 0.55
    margin-bottom: 0.2
    space-after-header: 0
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
---

```{=typst}
#let red = rgb("BA0C2F")
#let uga-black = rgb("111111")
#let colw = 12.55in
#let gutter = 0.42in
#let sec(title) = block(fill: red, width: 100%, inset: (x: 8pt, y: 4.5pt))[#text(fill: white, weight: 700, size: 16pt)[#title]]
#let sub(title) = [#v(5pt)#text(weight: 700, size: 11pt)[#title]#v(2pt)]
#let para(body) = block(width: 100%)[#text(size: 9.2pt)[#body]]
#let small(body) = text(size: 7.0pt)[#body]
#set text(font: "Arial", size: 9.2pt, fill: uga-black)
#set par(justify: true, leading: 0.53em)
#set list(indent: 8pt, body-indent: 5pt)

#grid(
  columns: (colw, colw, colw),
  column-gutter: gutter,
  align: top,

  [
    #sec[Introduction]
    #para[
      Seasonal influenza vaccination is the primary strategy to reduce influenza-related illness and death. Multiple vaccine types are available. Previous work has shown (1) vaccine formulation influences immune response [1–4] and (2) repeated annual vaccination can reduce antibody responses [5–7]. Optimal vaccine choice and sequencing remain uncertain and may vary by age. This study investigates how prior vaccine type affects subsequent H1N1 antibody responses across a broad adult population.
    ]

    #v(0.08in)
    #image("figures/original-assets/crops/picture-9.png", width: 100%)

    #v(0.08in)
    #sec[Methods]
    #sub[Data]
    #para[
      #list(
        [We analyzed data from two prior influenza vaccine studies: a longitudinal cohort (FluVac, 2013–2025) and a randomized clinical trial (URIV, 2015–2020).],
        [Across multiple U.S. sites, participants received various influenza vaccine formulations.],
        [Serum samples were collected pre- and post-vaccination, and antibodies were measured.],
      )
    ]
    #text(size: 8.2pt, weight: 700)[Table 1: Number of observations by prior and current vaccine.]
    #v(0.03in)
    #image("figures/original-assets/crops/picture-252.png", width: 94%)

    #sub[Statistical Analysis]
    #para[
      #list(
        [Multivariable linear regression was used to model antibody fold-change as a function of pre-titer, prior vaccine, age, current vaccine strain, and vaccine strain change.],
        [Models were fit by current vaccine group.],
      )
    ]
  ],

  [
    #sec[Univariable Analysis]
    #grid(columns: (1fr, 1fr), column-gutter: 0.12in,
      [#align(center)[#text(size: 10pt, weight: 700)[Fold-change]]],
      [#align(center)[#text(size: 10pt, weight: 700)[Post-vaccination titer]]],
    )
    #v(0.04in)
    #grid(columns: (1fr, 1fr), column-gutter: 0.10in, row-gutter: 0.06in,
      [#image("figures/original-assets/crops/picture-14.png", width: 100%)],
      [#image("figures/original-assets/crops/picture-44.png", width: 100%)],
      [#image("figures/original-assets/crops/picture-13.png", width: 100%)],
      [#image("figures/original-assets/crops/picture-42.png", width: 100%)],
      [#image("figures/original-assets/crops/picture-14.png", width: 100%)],
      [#image("figures/original-assets/crops/picture-44.png", width: 100%)],
    )
  ],

  [
    #sec[Multivariable Regression Analysis]
    #v(0.03in)
    #grid(columns: (1fr, 1fr), column-gutter: 0.10in, row-gutter: 0.07in,
      [#image("figures/original-assets/crops/picture-21.png", width: 100%)],
      [#image("figures/original-assets/crops/picture-24.png", width: 100%)],
      [#image("figures/original-assets/crops/picture-21.png", width: 100%)],
      [#image("figures/original-assets/crops/picture-24.png", width: 100%)],
    )

    #v(0.07in)
    #sec[Discussion]
    #para[
      Prior vaccination had modest effects on subsequent immune responses, both before and after adjusting for potential confounders.
      #list(
        [Prior FM vaccine was associated with higher post-titers and fold-change compared to other groups.],
        [Participants previously vaccinated with FB showed poorer fold-change across vaccine sequences.],
      )
      Fold-change and post-vaccination titer capture different aspects of immunity, and patterns were not always consistent across these metrics. Models that control for baseline titers are therefore important.
      #list(
        [FM–FZSD sequence showed a large fold-change, but had low baseline titers.],
        [FB–FB sequence showed a small boost but high post-titers.],
      )
      Responses varied by homologous vaccination history.
      #list(
        [FB–FB sequences show lower fold-change compared to other vaccination groups.],
        [FV–FV was comparable to FZSD–FV, while FZSD–FZSD responded better than FV–FZSD.],
      )
    ]

    #v(0.05in)
    #sec[References]
    #small[
      [1] B. J. Cowling et al., “Comparative Immunogenicity of Several Enhanced Influenza Vaccine Options for Older Adults,” Clin Infect Dis, 2020.\
      [2] S. Gouma et al., “Comparison of Human H3N2 Antibody Responses Elicited by Egg-Based, Cell-Based, and Recombinant Protein–Based Influenza Vaccines,” Clin Infect Dis, 2019.\
      [3] S. Zhong et al., “Influenza A(H3N2) Antibody Responses to Standard-Dose Versus Enhanced Influenza Vaccine Immunogenicity,” J Infect Dis, 2024.\
      [4] D. F. Hoft et al., “Comparisons of the Humoral and Cellular Immune Responses Induced by Live Attenuated and Inactivated Influenza Vaccine,” CVI, 2017.\
      [5] M.-H. Sung et al., “Assessment of Humoral Immune Responses to Repeated Influenza Vaccination in a Multiyear Cohort,” J Infect Dis, 2024.\
      [6] B. J. Cowling et al., “Dynamics of the Immune Responses to Repeat Influenza Vaccination Exposures,” Clin Infect Dis, 2024.\
      [7] S. Khurana et al., “Repeat vaccination reduces antibody affinity maturation across different influenza vaccine platforms in humans,” Nat Commun, 2019.
    ]
  ],
)
```
